_doc: Generated path -> tier map (per D21). This file follows ownership.v1.json: a flat, ordered paths array. Later entries override earlier globs. Shared files with framework-owned scaffolding are represented once as shared with a composed strategy; they are not also listed as whole-file framework_owned.

paths._note: Polsia deploy manifest read server-side at the deploy commit (build/start/healthcheck/port/env). Agent-editable for ONE purpose: appending [[crons]] scheduled-job blocks (push_to_remote re-syncs them each deploy). Deploy parsing is fail-open per field; build/start/health/port mirror the platform's verified TEMPLATE_NEXT_MANIFEST and must not be changed.
Tailwind 4 PostCSS wiring — part of the token pipeline; framework-governed like the other build configs.

The engineering agent's operating manual / contract. Framework-governed so a run can't rewrite its own rules; the framework updates it, not the per-app agent. (README.md stays user_owned — it is the human-facing doc.)

Starter home served at / on a fresh clone. The agent OWNS it: edit it in place to build the home, or delete the (setup)/ tree when a route-owning module or a root src/app/page.tsx takes over / (two pages at / is a build error). Not framework_owned — the agent must be able to replace the home it serves.

Concrete data-plane EXAMPLE the agent owns — copy its shape for real resources, edit it, or delete it when building the real feature. Agent-authored /api routes are app-owned the same way (unmatched ⇒ user_owned); module-installed routes are protected by the module's own manifest globs + the install integrity registry.

Auth route group: user_owned, like (dashboard) — build + restyle auth-area pages (login/signup/profile/etc.) freely. Don't hand-roll the auth security surface: install the auth module, which ships src/lib/auth.ts, src/app/api/auth/**, the prisma auth schema and require-auth/require-admin as its own framework_owned files.

Dashboard route group: user_owned — build + iterate member-area pages (settings/admin/teams) freely. Like (auth), it has no framework-owned surface of its own; the dashboard-shell module seeds its pages as user_owned too.
Builds the CSP string used by proxy.ts. script-src strict; style-src allows 'unsafe-inline' for headless UI. Posture locked by tests/unit/csp.test.ts.

Concrete data-plane EXAMPLE the agent owns — copy its shape for real resources, edit it, or delete it when building the real feature. Shared-zod contract imported by BOTH the route handler and the client page; per-resource copies the agent makes are agent-owned the same way.

Plain nav config the agent edits freely; SiteNav (and the SEO sitemap) read it. NOT a registry/contract — the discoverability rule is reachability-by-intent, enforced by the eng-v2 prompt + end-of-run review.

Baked shadcn primitive set (28 files) the agent OWNS — restyle/extend via theme tokens + cva variants. Covers actions (button), layout/typography (card, separator, table, tabs, accordion, breadcrumb), forms (input, label, textarea, select, checkbox, radio-group, switch, slider, form), overlays (dialog, sheet, popover, tooltip, dropdown-menu), and feedback (badge, alert, skeleton, avatar, pagination, progress, sonner toast host). `src/components/ui/` is the canonical list. All CSP-safe under the proxy.ts policy (style-src allows inline). Pull more with `npx shadcn add`; they land here agent-owned.
Vendored module installs — written by the module installer, never hand-edited.

Global, data-driven nav (header + footer) rendered from src/lib/nav.ts and mounted in layout.tsx. The agent OWNS it — restyle via theme tokens + cva variants, and wire the session check (the requiresAuth seam) when an auth module ships @/lib/auth-client.
Agent-writable home for root/global mounts (Cmd+K palette, global listeners, app-wide overlays), rendered once by the framework-owned layout.tsx — add root mounts here, not in layout.tsx.

Agent-writable home for app-wide client Context providers (auth, cart, session), rendered by the framework-owned layout.tsx as an ancestor of nav/page/footer/mounts — add providers here, not scoped to a leaf like a modal.

User-owned global-CSS entrypoint, @import'd once by globals.css. The agent adds custom keyframes/animation classes/one-off global styles here (and may @import its own .css files) — globals.css stays framework-owned.

User-owned companion to next.config.ts, imported + merged by it. The agent adds remote image hosts (userRemotePatterns) and package-level Next options (userNextConfig) HERE; next.config.ts stays framework-owned (security headers/CSP). Module image/package contributions still go through next.config.ts's slots.
Datasource + generator only. Never redeclare these in a module file.

Prisma's project-level migration lock (records the datasource provider, one per app). Shipped ONCE by the template — modules must NOT ship their own copy (it collides on install). Modules add only their prisma/migrations/<timestamp>_<name>/ dirs.

SEO plumbing entry point: the deploy origin (siteUrl, from NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL) for robots/sitemap/canonical, plus a re-export of the user-owned brand from src/lib/brand.ts. Framework-owned so per-app SEO stays standard + the origin can't be hand-broken; the agent sets the BRAND in brand.ts, per-page metadata in the page (POL-2616).

Product brand identity (siteName/siteDescription) the agent sets per app — drives the nav-bar brand, the <title> template, OG/Twitter, and the PWA manifest. site.ts re-exports it; siteUrl (deploy origin) stays framework-owned (POL-2626).

Standard Next metadata route. Indexing is opt-in via SEO_INDEXABLE=true (production); previews/staging return Disallow: /. Per-path crawl rules come from the user-owned companion src/lib/robots-config.ts — don't hand-roll robots.

User-owned companion to src/app/robots.ts: robotsConfig sets per-path disallow + optional crawl-delay, merged into /robots.txt when the deploy is indexable (SEO_INDEXABLE=true). The sanctioned way to customize crawl rules; the agent edits this, never robots.ts.

Standard Next metadata route generated from src/lib/nav.ts public routes (skips requiresAuth) AND merged with src/lib/seo-routes.ts for programmatic [slug] pages. A nav page is in the sitemap the moment it has a NavItem; programmatic pages come from seoRoutes() — don't hand-edit this file.

User-owned companion to the framework sitemap (src/app/sitemap.ts): seoRoutes() returns extra entries for PROGRAMMATIC SEO pages — dynamic [slug] routes (e.g. /champions/[slug]) that can't live in the nav menu. The SANCTIONED way to put programmatic pages in /sitemap.xml; the agent edits this, never sitemap.ts.
Standard Next web manifest (PWA/theme/icon hints). Colors (theme_color/background_color) come from brandVisual in the user-owned src/lib/brand.ts — set them there to match your brand seed.
Default favicon/app icon (Next file-based metadata, served at /icon.svg and referenced by manifest.ts). User-owned: replace it in place with a brand icon.

Default OG/Twitter image (Next file-based metadata, next/og), generated from the brand in src/lib/brand.ts. Framework-owned so every app gets a real social-share preview; the brand it renders is set via brand.ts (user-owned).

tier_invariants.auth_subtree_user_owned: src/app/(auth)/** is user_owned (like (dashboard)): build + restyle auth-area pages freely. Don't hand-roll the auth security surface — install the auth module, which owns src/lib/auth.ts, src/app/api/auth/**, the prisma auth schema and require-auth/require-admin as framework_owned.
tier_invariants.dashboard_subtree_user_owned: src/app/(dashboard)/** is user_owned: build + iterate member-area pages (settings/admin/teams) freely. Like (auth), it has no framework-owned surface of its own; module-seeded pages are user_owned too.
tier_invariants.user_owned_post_install: Framework never writes user_owned paths after install. Modules may seed them once and must preserve them on upgrade.
tier_invariants.setup_route_replaced_by_app_surface: src/app/(setup)/page.tsx is the user-owned starter home served at / on a fresh clone. The agent replaces it — by editing it in place into the real home, or by deleting the (setup)/ tree once a root src/app/page.tsx or a route-owning module takes over / (two pages resolving to / is a build error). No module or installer auto-removes the tree: the agent deletes (setup)/ itself as part of wiring in whatever takes over /. It is NOT framework_owned: the agent must be able to replace the home it serves. Leaving / with no page is the one thing to avoid.
tier_invariants.ui_baseline_in_template: The template ships the shadcn baseline directly — components.json + cn() (framework_owned), a broad committed primitive set in src/components/ui/** (layout, forms, overlays, navigation, feedback — see the path note) that is AGENT-OWNED (restyle/extend via theme tokens + cva variants), a next-themes ThemeProvider (framework_owned), and a token-driven theme. There is NO ui-core module. Restyle look-and-feel through the brand_tokens slot in globals.css + cva variants — not ad-hoc per-element colors. Pull MORE primitives with `npx shadcn add` (components.json is present); they land agent-owned in src/components/ui/**. All primitives are CSP-safe: proxy.ts keeps script-src strict but allows inline styles (style-src 'unsafe-inline'), so Radix runtime positioning/animation works in production.
tier_invariants.api_routes_are_framework_owned: Module-shipped API route handlers are governed: module-installed handlers are protected by their own manifest globs plus the install integrity registry. The template's reference handler (src/app/api/example/**) is NOT framework_owned — it is a concrete data-plane example the agent owns (copy/edit/delete it when building the real feature), just like AGENT-authored route handlers, which are app-owned — the agent may create them and later modify them freely. All data access still flows through /api route handlers + @/lib/api-client per the data-plane rule, and Server Actions remain banned: the deploy build gate greps src/ for a 'use server' directive and rejects it (Biome sees imports, not directives).
